title: Lastrea inaequalis
taxonomy: Lastrea inaequalis is classified within the family Thelypteridaceae. This species is part of the genus Lastrea. It belongs to the order Polypodiales and the class Polypodiopsida. As a fern, it is further classified as a vascular plant and a member of the division Pteridophyta. Additionally, it is also categorized as a member of the kingdom Plantae.
uses: Lastrea inaequalis, also known as the mountain fern or Japanese shield fern, is a plant species found in East Asia. While research is limited, some studies suggest that extracts from Lastrea inaequalis may have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. These properties could potentially be used to develop treatments for various health issues, such as wounds, skin conditions, and infections. However, further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Lastrea inaequalis as a medicinal agent. More studies are required to fully understand its potential applications in traditional medicine.

It is also believed to possess antimicrobial and antioxidant properties, making it effective in fighting off infections and protecting against cell damage. Additionally, Lastrea inaequalis may have a positive impact on cardiovascular health by reducing blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Furthermore, it has been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, such as constipation and diarrhea. More research is needed to fully understand the extent of its health benefits.

preparations: Lastrea inaequalis is a species of fern, and while its herbal preparations are not well-documented, traditional uses of ferns often involve them in teas, infusions, and topical applications. intro: Lastrea inaequalis, also known as the Asplenium inaequalis, is a type of fern species belonging to the Aspleniaceae family. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing oxidative stress and alleviating symptoms of various diseases. The bioactive constituents of Lastrea inaequalis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Lastrea inaequalis is commonly prepared into teas, infusions, and extracts, and is used to treat conditions such as fever, respiratory infections, and digestive issues.
